description_long: This is a tall, vertical pennant mounted on a dark metallic pole with a pointed spearhead finial at the top. The pole features a horizontal crossbar near the top, from which a translucent, white fabric banner hangs. The banner displays a detailed, symmetrical heraldic design, possibly an emblem or crest, rendered in a subtle, light color. The pole appears sturdy and is likely made of metal, while the banner is made of a lightweight fabric that drapes naturally. Decorative end caps are present on the crossbar, adding to the ornamental appearance. The overall design suggests it is intended for ceremonial or decorative use in an urban setting, such as a plaza, park, or near a public building.
description: A tall, spear-tipped metal pole supports a translucent white banner with a heraldic design, suspended from a horizontal crossbar. The pennant is ornate and ceremonial, suitable for display in public urban spaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full banner with its heraldic design and the spear-tipped pole.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the pole and the barely visible edge of the banner.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the banner and pole visible but the design less distinct.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the pole and the edge of the banner.
attribute_graphic_design: The banner features a symmetrical, heraldic-style emblem in a light color on a translucent white background. The design is ornate and central, with no visible text.
